title: GoogLeNet: Looking at Multiple Scales at Once
concept: GoogLeNet became famous for using Inception modules, where several filter sizes inspect the same feature map in parallel so the network can notice small, medium, and larger visual patterns at once.

exercises.prompt: Recap: If you replace a 3x3 filter with a 5x5 filter, what usually changes most?

options.text: The receptive field gets larger, so the filter can use a wider neighborhood
The image turns back into raw RGB pixels

exercises.explain: Right — larger filters see a wider patch. GoogLeNet's big idea was: why choose just one patch size?

cards.title: One object, many useful scales
cards.body: A cat photo contains tiny clues like whisker edges, medium clues like an eye shape, and larger clues like a whole face patch. If a CNN uses only one filter size at that moment, it may emphasize one scale and underuse the others.

cards.title: GoogLeNet's core idea
cards.body: GoogLeNet introduced the Inception module: instead of sending a feature map through just one filter size, it sends the same input through several branches in parallel — such as 1x1, 3x3, and 5x5 paths — so the network can examine multiple spatial scales at once.

cards.title: Three camera lenses on the same scene
cards.body: Imagine three friends looking at the same playground. One uses a zoom lens for tiny details, one uses a normal lens for medium objects, and one uses a wide lens for larger layouts. They all study the same scene, just at different scales. An Inception module does that with learned filters.

cards.title: Same feature map, different questions
cards.body: Suppose a feature map already highlights fur, eyes, and ears. A 1x1 branch can quickly remix channels at each pixel, a 3x3 branch can detect local parts like an eye corner, and a 5x5 branch can respond to a bigger face-like arrangement. The module does not guess which scale matters beforehand — it checks several.

cards.title: GoogLeNet did not just mean 'more layers'
cards.body: A common oversimplification is that famous CNNs only improved by getting deeper. GoogLeNet also got wider inside a module: multiple branches run side by side. The architecture innovation was not just depth, but deliberate multi-scale design.

cards.title: One path vs. multi-scale paths
cards.body: GoogLeNet's module asks several scale questions about the same input instead of betting on only one.

left.text:
One filter size only
One scale emphasized
Other scales must wait

right.text:
1x1, 3x3, 5x5 in parallel
Small + medium + larger clues
Same input, multiple scales

exercises.prompt: What made GoogLeNet's Inception idea stand out from a plain single-path CNN layer?

options.text: It ran multiple filter sizes on the same input in parallel
It removed convolutions and used only file compression
It forced every layer to use only 5x5 kernels

exercises.explain: Exactly — the same feature map fans out into several branches so different scales can be tested at once.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In an Inception-style module, the parallel branches usually start from the same incoming feature map.
exercises.explain: True — the branches are alternative views of the same input, not four unrelated images.

exercises.sentence: Different filter sizes let an Inception module look at multiple visual ___ at once.
exercises.explain: Right — the whole point is multi-scale processing.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best captures the mental model of GoogLeNet's parallel branches?

options.text: Several filter sizes inspect the same feature map at once so the network can notice small, medium, and larger patterns together
Each branch gets a different training set and never compares notes
Parallel branches mean the CNN skips spatial pattern finding entirely

exercises.explain: Yes — think 'same input, several scale-sensitive paths,' not 'several disconnected models.'

exercises.prompt: Why is looking at multiple scales at once useful for an image model?

options.text: Because meaningful visual clues can appear as tiny textures, medium parts, or larger arrangements, so parallel branches can cover more of that range at the same time
Because CNNs are unable to learn anything unless every branch uses the same filter size
Because larger filters automatically replace the need for channels
Because parallel branches convert classification into image compression

exercises.explain: Exactly — images contain useful structure at several sizes, and GoogLeNet's module was designed to inspect that range in one step. Next: the exact Inception module layout.
